## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, so now I want to talk about the quick launch. And the quick launch is a great way to bring in money fast, hence the name quick launch. So the deal with the quick launch, the caveat is that you do need to have an offer, some number of product and an offer, and you do need to have a list. So this isn't for people who are just starting out. If you're just starting out, then you want to go with the seed launch. But the quick launch, if you've got that offer and the list, it can be just, well, very, very quick way to bring in some momentum and some money into your business. And also to get people to jump from, you know, from that, into that buyer column, to move them from the prospect list to the buyer list. So what you need is basically a story and some type of a special offer. So the special offer doesn't have to be complicated. It can be some type of a discount. It could be an extra bonus. It could be an extra tele-seminar. Just it really doesn't have to be fancy. One very simple one, you know, if you're sitting there trying to rack your brain, what is it? 50% off. Your offer can be great and give you a great quick launch if you can swing it. If you have that margin where you can give a 50% off discount, or if you've got a great back-end offer so that after they purchase, you're going to have some type of an upsell for them either immediately or down the road. And so that can make sense. But if you do 50% off, that's great. It doesn't have to be. I've done many, many quick launches that were like, you know, 20% off and did really well. So you need some type of a special offer. You can also just do bundle stuff together. Bundles work really well. Take your main product and bundle in something else as a bonus or put them together into a super bundle. That works great for a quick launch offer. Now next you need that story. The story doesn't have to be amazing. The classic example that I've taught, and I've been teaching this one for years, and I'm going to keep on teaching it for years because it works really, really well. It worked well for me and it works really, really well for my product launch formula owners is my tax sale. I'll be teaching that in one of the upcoming videos. And the story behind this, I'll go into detail, but the basic premise of this was that I got a big unexpected tax bill. My business did really well. I took my taxes to the accountant. He gave them back to me and I was like, oh, you got to be kidding. Actually he gave, he told me the numbers over the phone and there was like, he's reading off them one digit at a time and then he read an extra digit. There's a whole extra digit. So it was not a happy day. But then I came up with the idea, I was like, oh my gosh, what am I going to do? And frankly, I had the money to pay for it, but I didn't want to write this huge check. So I put together a special offer and did this super quick launch, super quick promotion. At the end of the day, this is just a promotion. It's almost like a sale.

## [03:00 - 06:03]

And it worked really well. And so that was the birth of the quick launch. The funny thing about that is that I made a lot of money with my quick launch. And then in teaching it to product launch formula owners over the years, they've just made, you know, we added up all the money that that tax bill has generated for all my PILF owners, you know, it's hundreds of thousands of dollars. So it's awesome. Quick launches are very cool. Now the story, your story doesn't have to be about a tax bill. It can be about any number of things. It doesn't have to be all that amazing. It could be about special events like weddings or birthdays or graduations or you got a new office or a new home. It could be about other financial calamities like I, you know, I had a tax sale. I mean, you could have your basement flooded or your pipes broke or or whatever. It could be something like I need to raise money. And that was the story of my tax sale. I just went out and told people I need to raise money. That's why I'm discounting this. So or the other thing you could do is it could just be a simple customer appreciation or a subscriber appreciation or a reader appreciation where you go out and you say, Hey, thanks for being one of my readers. You know, I really appreciate you. And because of that, I put together this amazing special deal. You know, you do have to have a start and an end to the launch like you do with every launch of it. And that's what gets people moving. So you have that special offer. You have a reason for the special offer. You have a start and an end and you and you promote it. You mail people. Generally, a quick launch is all done via email. So that makes it super, super simple. Now one thing about quick launches is you're not going to do a million dollars with one of these. It's just not the nature of them, but it's a really nice cash infusion and it moves people from that buyer column or from the prospect column, from your prospect list over to your buyer list. If you remember, back when I was talking about lists, there's different types of lists. There's those and two of them are that prospect list and the buyer's list. Well, if you can get someone to buy something from you and move them into that buyer column, onto that buyer list, they are worth 15 times more to you. And that number varies from market to market and business to business, but I've seen that over and over my business. It's between 15 and 20 times more valuable to have someone on that buyer list. If you have a thousand leads or a thousand people on your buyer list and a thousand people on your prospect list and you send each of them a promotion, that buyer list is going to generate 15 times more dollars in sales. If nothing else, doing a quick launch, moving people over to that buyer list is super, super powerful, but it's just also a really great way to bring in money. Quick launches are very cool because they're simple, they're easy, they're generally just email and all you have to do is put together that special offer and put together, create that story behind it and then have that start and end to the launch and you win.

## [06:03 - 08:26]

One of the things, the way I structure this, the way it allows you to do is it allows you to, one of the things, I remember the first time I went furniture shopping and really since we were so just desperately poor for so, when I was newly married and our kids were little, I can remember vividly when I went furniture shopping for the very first time and it wasn't that long ago before that, it was all hand-me-downs, but in any case, we went to this one furniture store and they were having this big sale and we were just like all the, you know, went throughout the whole store and everything in the store was on sale, I had this sale ticket or whatever and I thought, well, this is really amazing, but since it was the first time we bought furniture, I just was not ready and my wife and I were not ready to make these big decisions because it was such a big decision and so we held off and we didn't, you know, the sale ended on like whatever Tuesday and we didn't buy anything and then we waited a couple weeks and we went back to that same store and they were having another sale, a different sale and everything in the entire store was on sale and we thought, that's sort of weird and because we take so long to make decisions about purchases like that, we held off again and we went back two weeks later and they were having another sale and everything was marked off and we quickly realized that this store, everything was always on sale and you don't want to have, in my opinion, you don't want a business where everything is always on sale because then, or we're just frequently having sales like every month because then people are going to be looking at your business and say, I'm interested in buying this, but I'm just going to wait a little while because I know it's going to go on sale. I don't ever want to have that kind of positioning. Remember, we talked about positioning earlier. You don't want to ever have the kind of positioning where people just expect discounts from you and they are just going to wait for a sale. You don't want to continually do these quick launches and that is also the reason why you want to go out and have a story and a reason behind the discount. You want there to be a good, solid reason for it that justifies that special deal that you're giving people. So that's just something to think about with your quick launches. But again, they're simple, they're easy, they're actually fun, they're lucrative and they move people from being prospects to being buyers. So with that said, we're going to get to some examples now.
